mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS declines or stays flat after a tightening peak, equity discount rates stop rising and valuation pressure on the S&P 500 eases, producing positive 20-day forward returns. The signal fails if policy rates keep rising or if equities stop reacting to lower funding costs.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods where FEDFUNDS is unchanged or falling for at least one month, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 55% in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% suppresses risk appetite by keeping short-term financing costs restrictive; under that threshold the market should show weaker forward returns, while a move below the threshold should improve 5-day returns as traders price an easier policy path.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 5-day forward returns are not negative more often than positive when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or if the triggered subset does not underperform the unconditional mean by at least 0.2 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS indicates labor-market deterioration that compresses earnings expectations and increases recession odds, pushing S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The interaction matters because unemployment alone without payroll weakness should not carry the same signal.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS growth is negative over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered cases do not show negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of observations, or if the joint condition performs no better than UNRATE alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS reflects stronger consumption and revenue growth for listed firms, which should support higher S&P 500 returns over a quarter. The signal fails if stronger retail sales are not followed by improved equity performance after allowing for normal reporting lag.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not above their unconditional median following 3-month RSAFS growth acceleration, or if the relationship is negative in more than two consecutive rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO together with rising HOUST signals stronger real activity and cyclical demand for goods, which should lift equity prices over the next month. The effect is strongest when industrial output and housing start moving higher at the same time; divergent moves should weaken the signal.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-momentum condition does not outperform each single proxy alone, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods where both INDPRO and HOUST rise over their recent baselines.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in GDPC1 after prior weakness marks a growth rebound that should improve earnings expectations and support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This mechanism breaks if GDP revisions reverse the apparent rebound or if market participants have already fully priced the recovery.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive after at least one weak quarterly reading
failure_condition: Reject if quarter-ahead SPX returns do not exceed baseline following a positive GDP inflection, or if the sign accuracy is below 55% in post-recession expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS stops growing while UNRATE begins to rise, labor income momentum stalls and recession risk increases, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal requires the conjunction of payroll weakness and unemployment deterioration; either alone is insufficient.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth is flat or negative and UNRATE rises by at least 0.1pp over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not yield negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the joint condition is not more predictive than either labor series by itself.

mechanism_narrative: In an inflation pressure regime, a rising CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS below the inflation trend implies real rates are still too low and policy will tighten further, which should hurt S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails when inflation is stable or when policy is already restrictive enough to offset the surprise.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered cases do not underperform untriggered cases over 20 days, or if negative SPX returns occur in fewer than 60% of instances when inflation runs more than 1.0pp above the policy rate.
